column1: Figure  1  Preparation  of  6-mercaptopurine-loaded  nanomedicines  (6-MPNs)  and  schematic  of  6-MPNs  augmenting  the  anticancer  efficiency  after  oral  administration.
6-MPNs were constructed via a double-emulsion solvent evaporation method. The inner water phase (W1) containing 6-MP and PVA was added dropwise in the oil phase
containing PLGA (O) to get the primary emulsion (W1/O). Then the W1/O was dispersed in the outer water phase (W2) to obtain double emulsion (W1/O/W2), which
was evaporated, centrifuged and washed to purify the nanomedicines. The 6-MPNs are capable of improving the solubility of 6-MP and increasing the intestinal absorption,
thereby enhancing the anticancer effect and reducing chemotherapy-induced systemic toxicities.
